title: 1.1: Shows the IP checksum value within a complete packet hex dump (e.g., 0x0D0F at bytes 10-11)
1.2: Shows the TCP checksum value inside a packet hex dump at bytes 36–37 (e.g., 0x6F16 at bytes 36-37)
1.3: Explains that Scapy recalculates checksums on each bytes() call rather than returning cached values
1.4: Reports which byte positions differ between the two hex outputs after payload modification (e.g., positions 36 and 40)
1.5: States that assigning 0xAAAA after the first build produces identical behavior to setting it before build (e.g., both produce 0xAAAA in final bytes)
1.6: Shows complete original packet bytes both before and after modifying the copy to demonstrate isolation (e.g., using copy.deepcopy())
1.7: Reports the decimal value of the IP length field after growing the packet (e.g., 55 decimal)
1.8: Reports that the IP length field matches the actual byte count with zero discrepancy (e.g., both show 55 bytes)
1.9: Identifies that IP checksum remains unchanged when only payload is modified (e.g., stays at 0x0D0F)
1.10: Identifies that TCP checksum changes when payload is modified (e.g., from 0x6F16 to 0x6E16)
1.11: Shows that manually assigned checksum value 0xAAAA survives into the final wire bytes.
1.12: Shows complete hex dump starting from byte offset 0 for the deep copy isolation scenario (eg: 45 00 00 30 00 01..)
